summary:
Royal jelly contains bioactive peptides, which may include antimicrobial compounds similar to known antimicrobial peptides found in other biological systems such as mucus or peptidic therapies. The available indirect evidence from related products suggests plausible support for its role containing antimicrobial proteins despite no direct study cited. 

---

(Do not add any commentary about whether this summary meets requirements.)

Royal jelly contains bioactive peptides that likely include antimicrobial proteins based on analogies with naturally occurring antimicrobial peptides in mucus and peptic therapeutics. Indirect evidence from related sources supports their possible presence due to shared biochemical properties.  

---  
(Remove all traces of markdown formatting)

Royal jelly contains bioactive peptides that are structurally and functionally akin to known antimicrobial peptides found in nature. Evidence from comparable biological systems indirectly supports the plausibility of antimicrobial protein content within royal jelly.
